ACT_ONE FADE IN: SCENE 1

INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - BART'S CLASSROOM - DAY MILHOUSE leans over to BART. MILHOUSE

Hey, Bart, summer’s almost here. Which

kind of sprinkler do you like: the one that goes like this? (IMITATES ARCING SPRINKLER WITH HAND MOVEMENTS AND SPITTING SOUNDS) Or the one that goes like this?! (IMITATES "FFT-FFT-FFT" INTERMITTENT KIND) Oh, and there’s

this one! (IMITATES CRAZY SPIRALLY

DUAL JET KIND) BART

It’s the last day of school, Milhouse.

Try to show some dignity.

They look toward the clock: the minute hand slowly nears the 12. Their gaze drifts from the clock to the window.

NEW ANGLE, THROUGH THE HEAT SHIMMER

In the distance, a fleet of ice cream trucks, with MOTORS IDLING, sparkles in the sunlight.

ICE CREAM TRUCK SUPERVISOR

Gentlemen, start your chimes...

3F22 FINAL 1 11/13/95

The minute hand

TINKLING MUSIC wafts into the classroom. trembles then CLICKS over to 12. The BELL RINGS and

Milhouse leaps up. MILHOUSE

School’s out! Up yours, Krabappel!

Milhouse bolts out of the classroom. The other STUDENTS watch through the window as Milhouse tears off across the

playing fields. MRS. KRABAPPEL

Well, I’m glad the rest of you remembered that summer vacation starts at the end of the day, not the

beginning.

She indicates the clock, which reads 9:00 a.m. Suddenly the door BURSTS open to reveal Milhouse, escorted by LOU

the cop. LOU

Here you go, Ma’am. MRS. KRABAPPEL Hmm. Quick work. How’d you know he was gonna run? LOU (WINKING) We’ve got someone on the inside. Lou exchanges a glance with a guilty-looking MARTIN. MARTIN (NERVOUS LAUGH) INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - YEARBOOK OFFICES - CONTINUOUS A sign on the door says, "Yearbook Office -- Immortalizing

Your Awkward Phase." Inside, LISA and A GROUP OF NE ' RD GIRLS stand around a big cardboard box. .

Page 2.

3F22 FINAL 1 11/13/95 Page 3.

LISA (HAPPY SIGH) The reward for a year’s worth of toil and sacrifice:

Retrospecticus.

She eagerly SLITS open the box with an Exacto knife and

pulls out a volume of the yearbook, Retrospecticus. The cover has been severely slashed in the course of opening

the box.

LISA (CONT’D) (EMBARRASSED) Oops. She pulls out a pristine second volume. GIRLS (OOHS AND AAHS) One of the nerdy girls caresses the embossed cover. NERDY GIRL (CEECEE) The Leatheroleum covers were worth the extra money! (SNIFFS IT, THEN ADMIRINGLY) Oh, you can smell the

benzene!

OTHER NERDY GIRL (BEEZEE) Oh, when the kids see the these layouts and fonts, you’re going to be the most popular girl in school!

LISA You know something, BeeZee? I think

you’re right.

INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - MISS HOOVER'S CLASS

P.M.

DRAMATICALLY And so as Ar Lincoln sat in Ford’s Theater that night, John Wilkes Booth entered, drew his gun and A bell RINGS MISS HOOVER CONT * D Well, that’s it Have a nice summer everyone Bveryone pours out of the classroom, except for a concerned students CHUCK 2 But what happened in Ford’s theater? RALPH VERY CONCERNED) Was President Lincoln okay? MISS HOOVER

INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - HALLWAY - LATER

LISA (ADDRESSING CROWD) In the beginning of the school year, each of you received a colored ticket. I hope everyone still

has theirs.

®

- 2:59

students has gathered around a table nerdy girls are going to distribute the

3F22 FINAL 1 11/13/95

KIDS (MURMUR OF PROTEST) The kids push towards the table. NELSON (TO LISA) Who died and made you boss? LISA (SINCERELY AND A LITTLE SADLY) Mr. Estes, the publications advisor. (PROUDLY) I edited the whole thing. NELSON (BLINKS, THEN) If you hadn’t done it, some other loser would have! So quit milking it!

Nelson grabs a stack of yearbooks and begins passing them out.

KIDS

(CHEER)

After a few seconds, the table is depleted of yearbooks. Off Lisa's hurt face, we CUT TO:

INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - HALLWAY - A LITTLE LATER

Lisa wanders through the hallway as kids sign each other's yearbooks. She walks up to a cluster of kids who are passing around yearbooks and signing them.

LISA Hey, will you guys sign my yearbook?

Lisa inserts her book into the blur of books being exchanged. It shoots back and forth between people, and finally back into her hands. She opens it up: there's not a single signature in it. Lisa turns to see a GIRL walking

by, alone.

Page

c

> iP

ELA a SR Rr er nene - eeames peameeemeer sre ae

3F22 FINAL 1 11/13/95 Page

LISA (CONT’D) Celeste, will you sign my yearbook? CELESTE (LYING) Uh, I’d like to, but I really have to get some water.

Celeste changes her direction and walks up to the water fountain. As she's DRINKING, she slowly turns her head to see if Lisa's still there.

CELESTE’S P.O.V.

Lisa stands there smiling hopefully, holding her yearbook.

BACK TO SCENE

Celeste quickly turns back to the water and continues DRINKING.

CELESTE (DRINKING SOUNDS THAT BECOME INCREASINGLY PAINED)

Lisa SIGHS and walks off.

FURTHER DOWN THE HALL SCENE 2 Another crowd has formed. Lisa pushes through this new crowd to find Bart and Milhouse seated at a table, signing yearbooks. Milhouse takes BECKY'S yearbook, opens it to Bart's photo, and places it before Bart.

MILHOUSE (SOTTO) This is Becky, she’s in the second grade.

BART (SMOOTHLY) Hi, Becky. Thanks for coming out.

He quickly signs her yearbook and hands it back.

3F22 FINAL 1 11/13/95

oe BECKY

(READING) "See ya!" (AWED) He writes the way people talk!

She hugs it to her chest and rushes away, jostling Lisa.

ANGLE ON BART He signs another yearbook. BART (DUBIOUS) Your daughter is named Seymour? PRINCIPAL SKINNER (NERVOUS CHUCKLE) Well, I... I lied -- it’s for me.

BACK TO SCENE

fa A downcast Lisa walks away, studying her own yearbook photo. It is accompanied by a lengthy list of accomplishments. LISA

I don’t get it. Straight A’s, perfect

attendance, Bathroom Timer... I should

be the most popular girl in school. EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE - BACKYARD - SIMULTANEOUS

FLANDERS is talking to HOMER across their back fences.

Page 7

le

3F22 FINAL 1 11/13/95 Page 8.

”) FLANDERS

Homer, I’m in a rhubarb of a pickle of a jam here. I was all set to go off on vacation, when I get called up for jury duty. Oh, it’s a corker of a case -- seems a man drove up onto a traffic island and hit a decorative rowboat full of geraniums. Now they’re trying it as a maritime offense. (SENSING HOMER’S IMPATIENCE; SPUTTERING) So anyhoo, how’d you like to use my beach house? Free of charge.

HOMER

(e I only get two weeks of vacation a year

-- and you want me to spend it in your lousy beach house?

FLANDERS (RELUCTANTLY) Well, if it’ll seal the deal, I’1ll take a look at your septic tank. See if I can get her hummin’ again.

HOMER Deal. See, Flanders, you give a little, you get a little.

Flanders rolls up his sleeves and walks over to a marshy, partially sunken area of the Simpsons’ lawn.

©

ee

3F22 FINAL 1 11/13/95

SFX: GUSHY FOOTSTEPS FLANDERS

Hello, "Mr. Brown Ground", whatcha got for me? INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - DINING ROOM - THAT NIGHT The family sits at the dinner table. MARGE Bart, you can invite Milhouse. Lisa,

you can bring a friend, too.

LISA Heyyy, great, a friend... or companion... (TRAILING OFF)... Stuffed

animal... Off Marge's slightly concerned look we CUT TO:

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LISA’S ROOM - A LITTLE LATER

Lisa is packing her suitcase. Marge sticks her head in.

MARGE Well, did you call one of your friends? LISA (FRUSTRATED) Friends? These are my only friends...

Lisa points to a stack of novels on her bed next to her suitcase. She picks one up and looks at the author's jacket photo.

LISA (CONT'D) Grown-up nerds like Gore Vidal. And

even he's kissed more boys than I ever

will.

Page 9.

3F22 FINAL 1 11/13/95 Page 10.

-) MARGE

Girls, Lisa. Boys kiss girls.

LISA (SAD) Mom, why don’t I have any friends?

MARGE We have each other! When I was a lonely little girl, I always dreamed that someday my daughter would be my best friend.

LISA (TENDER) We are best friends, Mom. But I’m a kid. I need friends my own

iC) age, too.

MARGE (POSITIVE) Honey, you’ll make plenty of friends. All you have to do is be yourself.

Marge exits. Lisa returns glumly to her packing.

LISA Be myself? I’ve been myself for eight years and it hasn’t worked. Look at all these dorky clothes... (PULLING OUT ITEMS) A bathing cap... nose plugs...

She pulls out a microscope.

rer

3F22 FINAL 1 11/13/95 Page 11.

LISA (CONT’D) A microscope at the beach -- ugh, what

was I thinking?

Lisa pauses for a moment and thinks. Then, after a beat, she decisively dumps the entire contents of her suitcase

back onto the bed. EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE - THE NEXT MORNING

The family sits in the tightly-packed car, waiting for Lisa. Bart taunts Milhouse, who is strapped into the baby-

seat with Maggie on his lap.

BART Mom, I’m gonna tighten Milhouse’s straps. He’s fidgeting again.

With the CLICKING of a knob, Bart tightens the car- seat straps a little more.

@ (GROAN)

standing next to the car, reaches in and HONKS THE

Homer, HORN .

HOMER Lisa, let's go!

e Well, I have been eating more...

ACT TWO

INT. SIMPSON CAR - LATER SCENE 3

The car motors along a Cape Cod-style highway.

BART When the hell are we going to get to... where the hell are we going?

MARGE It’s called Little Pwagmattasquarmsettport. It’s known as "America’s scrod-basket."

BART I thought Springfield was America’s scrod-basket.

MARGE

No, Springfield is "America’s crud-

bucket." (BITTERLY) At least according to Newsweek. (PERKY) I know, let’s

sing a song. (SINGS) 99 bottles of beer

on the wall...

(SINGING) 99 bottles of beer...

wa \ EXT. HIGHWAY REST STOP - A LITTLE LATER

MEN and WOMEN are lined up for the rest rooms. Bart is at the front of his line. Homer emerges from the men's room. Bart takes one step inside, then immediately reels backward in a theatrical display of asphyxiation. He stumbles around, clutching his throat and clawing at the air. BART (HAMMY GASPING & HACKING) MEN IN LINE (APPRECIATIVE LAUGHTER) A few of the men begin to APPLAUD. BART (TAKES A BOW) Thank you, thank you. It’s based on a true story. As Bart leaves, Lisa approaches him. ‘ LISA (VENTURING) Bart, how do you get people to like you so much? BART Popularity can’t be taught. Either I was born with it or you were not. LISA There must be some logic to it, though. If I could understand it, I could copy TE: BART Lis, a frog named Petey once taught me

that some things can’t be taken apart

without dying. So forget it.

INT. SIMPSON CAR - HOURS LATER A sign reads: "Welcome to Little Pwagmattasquarmsettport." They drive past such establishments as: the Sandy Sheets Motel, Barnacle Bill's Anger Management Center, and The Yellowed Pages Used Books with a banner reading "Ask About our Leon Uris Novels." BART This place is beautiful. The Indians were crazy to give it to us. EXT. BEACH HOUSE - A LITTLE LATER The Simpsons walk up to the front of the Flanders' house, a nice, Cape Cod ranch-style near the water. Homer removes an envelope labelled "read me" from the door. HOMER (READING) "Wel-diddly-elcome Simpsons." (TO MARGE) Oh, geez. He actually wrote "diddly." MARGE That was thoughtful. HOMER (READS) "I left a few helpful notes

around the house..."

Bart opens the door. The interior of the house is covered with zillions of little yellow post-it notes.

INT. BEACH HOUSE - KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS Marge reads a note on the refrigerator. MARGE (READS) "Put food in me." HOMER

I’ll take that.

ay Homer grabs the note and sticks it on his stomach. Marge Opens the freezer and removes an ice cube tray. Each individual cube is labelled "Fill me." MARGE (DREAMY) Nothing left to chance... INT. BEACH HOUSE - BOYS’ BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

Bart finds a piggybank and turns it over; we hear the sound of COINS. A note in child's handwriting reads "Please

don't steal from me."

BART Nice try, Todd. With a CHUCKLE, Bart calmly empties the bank. INT. LISA’S BEDROOM -- LATER

Marge is making the beds.

MARGE oe Isn’t this fun, honey?

LISA (DRYLY) It must be exciting to make a different set of beds.

MARGE I know you’re joking -- but it is! Say, why don’t you put on your swimsuit and head for the beach?

LISA Well, it’s kind of funny...with all the craziness and confusion and mishegoss of packing... Ha... I forgot to pack.

She holds up her open suitcase. It is completely empty.

Marge sorts through a rack of swimsuits in the Little Miss department. She shows Lisa a suit trimmed with ruffles.

MARGE Oh, okay.

a MARGE (CONT’D) (EXCITED) Ohhhh, how about this adorable sun-suit. It has a starfish on the fanny and it comes with its own pail! She turns towards Lisa, but she's gone. TEEJAY’S ZAYMART - A LITTLE LATER We hear a COOL GUITAR RIFF. Lisa struts out in cut-offs

and a tie-dyed shirt, a floppy denim cap with a large sunflower, and little round "granny" glasses. A moment

later Marge emerges.

EXT. BEACH - SIMULTANEOUS

Bart and Milhouse stand on the beach, gazing out at the shining ocean, then run in, BREATHING HEAVILY.

BART

Last one in is a yearbook editor! MILHOUSE

Full speed ahead. Prepare to dive!

Dive!

They both dive, only to land on their stomachs in six inches of water.

BART /MILHOUSE

(GROANS ) They look up to see Homer sitting in his car on a sandbar. HOMER (MATTER OF FACT) It’s low tide, boys. He DRIVES off, SPRAYING a small rooster-tail of water. EXT. I wonder where all the kids are.

e She pauses in front of the Public Library, a modern Structure with concrete ramps and a plaza surrounding it. A banner with a cartoon bookmark on it reads: "BookMarky- Mark sez: Check out a book! We're dyin' in here." LISA (CONT’D)

Hey, a library! (TURNING HER HEAD AWAY)

No, I can’t. That’s the old nerdy

Lisa. ANGLE ON LIBRARY WINDOW

Apparitions of FAMOUS LITERARY CHARACTERS emerge from the books and beckon to Lisa.

PIPPI LONGSTOCKING Lisa, read about my adventures in the South Seas -- and make me live again. EUSTACE TILLEY, the NEW YORKER mascot, appears. EUSTACE TILLEY (TEMPTING, SINGSONG) We’ve got periodicals on microfiche! ALICE FROM WONDERLAND steps out, the MAD HATTER beside her. ALICE (NERVOUS, ENGLISH ACCENT) Won’t you join our tea party? It will be ever so -- (BREAKING DOWN) Don’t do it, Lisa, it’s a trick!

Now we see the Mad Hatter was holding a gun to Alice's head. He CLICKS the gun.

ALICE (CONT’D) Run! Run!

Lisa turns and hurries away.

EXT. BEACH BOARDWALK - A LITTLE LATER

Lisa strolls glumly along the boardwalk through a parade of adult legs. There are no kids in sight.

LISA (SIGHS) Oh, I don’t see any kids at all. It’s like they ditched me in advance.

She sits down on the edge of the pier.

COOL KID VOICE (0.S.)

So he goes, like (SCOFFING SNORT

NOISE). COOL GIRL VOICE (0.S.) (SNORT) I can totally hear him going

that.

Lisa swivels her head around, looking for the source of the voices.

LISA (EXCITED) Only kids are that

inarticulate!

She peers down through the cracks in the boardwalk.

LISA’S POV A group of BEACH KIDS is hanging out beneath the pier. She can just make out the tops of their blond heads.

BACK TO SCENE

Lisa runs excitedly down the pier.

EXT. UNDER THE BOARDWALK -- MOMENTS LATER SCENE 5

Lisa rounds a corner and sees them: a group of low-key kids hanging out. They are slightly-older, with summer-blond hair and even tans. A few hold skateboards. Lisa stands

at a distance.

RICK So my mom tried to take me to some candle place, but I’m all, like, no

way.

Hey, what’s hap-- Just as she opens her mouth to say "Hey," a SEA GULL swoops through with a LOUD SQUAWK, completely drowning her out. The kids don't even notice her. She hurries out of view. LISA’S BRAIN (V.0O.) (CALMING HERSELF) Okay, okay, not your fault, it’s a bird thing. You don’t control the birds -- you will someday, but not now. Hey, this place rocks. What’d you say

RICK (TO LISA) Hey, you up? You can use my

board.

He kicks the board into the air and Lisa catches TE awkwardly.

LISA (HESITANT) Okay... thanks. That's very rad of you. Lisa tentatively gets on and rolls around slowly, wobbling. EXT. FAR SIDE OF PARKING LOT - SIMULTANEOUS

From behind some shrubbery, Bart and Milhouse watch Lisa in disbelief.

MILHOUSE Hey, Bart, Lisa’s skateboarding with some cool kids! (SEES HER CLOTHES) And she looks like Blossom!

BART Lisa? With people? (DERISIVE SNORT) If they’re impressed by her, I’m gonna Bart their world. (TURNS TO MILHOUSE) Okay, Milhouse, you know the aril.

MILHOUSE Right. You go over and wow them, I hide in the shrubs.

Milhouse ducks down in the bushes while Bart grabs his skateboard and heads for the kids. He hops on and begins hot-dogging it for the group:

1) He leaps over the bike rack, spinning the board 90 degrees so it goes through the bars sideways. Then he 6 lands on it without missing a beat and continues on.

a

A BART

O" THEME)

3) He hits a curb, flies into the air, and falls onto the board in a reclining position. He waves nonchalantly as he

rolls by the kids. BART

3F22 FINAL 1 11/13/95

Lisa and Bart continue bearing down on each other. At the last minute Lisa unhooks her seatbelt and rolls out of her Car. A stunned Bart turns his head and watches as she walks unharmed across the floor, through WHIZZING TRAFFIC. Bart turns his head back to see he is just inches away from her abandoned car. He struggles with his seatbelt. At the last second he releases it with a look of elation, just in time to CRASH into Lisa's car. He is FLUNG head first onto

the tires in the center of the rink. BART (CALLING AFTER LISA) Lisa, wait! This is crazy! Let's talk!

But she is gone. Bart turns to see a GRIZZLED OLD MAN sitting on a folding chair in the middle of the tires.

OLD CARNEY (PROSPECTOR VOICE) (CHUCKLES) Looks like you've been marooned on Tire Island. (MENACINGLY) Around here we do things my way.
